rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace the first invalid identifier on the same line and resolve it or remove the clause.
generic_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers should be addressed by ensuring all references are valid within the current scope.
AGREE contracts must only reference visible symbols or helpers declared in the same annex to avoid resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must reference valid AADL features or declared helpers.
When a guarantee or assumption references an identifier, that identifier must be resolvable in the current scope or declared as a helper.
anti_patterns: Adding new architecture elements to resolve AGREE reference errors; instead, fix or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE annex placement or assume/guarantee syntax
Component type vs implementation annex distinctions
